normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 1.3 - Waste, Recycling and Food Organics Garden Organics (FOGO) Collections for Multiple Dwellings, Mixed Use Developments and Non-Residential Developments
scope_summary: Local planning policy regulating waste, recycling and FOGO collection design for multiple dwellings, mixed use developments and non-residential developments in the City of Melville. Every planning application for these development types must be accompanied by a Waste Management Plan, and developments must incorporate bin storage sized using prescribed waste-generation rates, bin compound construction standards, and on-site or on-street collection requirements including truck access geometry. Includes a pro-forma Waste Management Plan template as an appendix.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: multiple dwellings (per dwelling, 3 bedrooms or greater)

key_numeric_standards.value: 1 week organics; 1-2 weeks waste and recycling

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: bin compounds in basement or under-croft levels
external bin compounds not within building

key_numeric_standards.value: not within front or secondary street setback area

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: bin compound access point with self-closing gate
structures over which collection truck travels

key_numeric_standards.topic: design vehicle length for on-site manoeuvring (forward entry/egress, max three-point turn)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: on-site waste collection truck manoeuvring and ramp/driveway gradients

key_numeric_standards.topic: bin collection point distance from truck laydown

key_numeric_standards.value: 4 m2 minimum or 0.5 m2 per dwelling, whichever is greater

cross_references.cited: State Planning Policy 7.3 Residential Design Codes Volume 1
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
Waste Avoidance and Resource Recovery Act 2007
AS 1668.2 The Use of Ventilation and Air Conditioning in Buildings
AS 2890.2 Parking Facilities: Off-Street Commercial Vehicle Facilities

quality_flags: Table 3 (bin selection matrix) lost its checkmark cells in extraction; which bin sizes apply to which streams must be read from the surrounding prose
minor OCR artifacts (e.g. 'suitable l storage' cl 4.1; stray '960' appended to cl 4.4)
references section cites 'Local Government Act 2015' which appears to be a source typo for the Local Government Act 1995 cited in the policy body
manifest instrument_name 'Waste and Recyclables Collection' is the older short title; current title adds FOGO - same instrument (LPP 1.3)
